dataSourceNote: This session's network egress policy again blocked the normal data pipeline: direct requests to query1.finance.yahoo.com, news.google.com, and www.sec.gov all returned policy-level 403s at the proxy (proxy status endpoint's recentRelayFailures, kind: connect_rejected). A WebFetch retry (which does not route through this session's local proxy) also got a 403 directly from Yahoo, indicating Yahoo itself blocks that path too. As a fallback, current price/news context below was gathered via WebSearch instead of scripts/fetch-market-data.mjs + fetch-news.mjs + fetch-fundamentals.mjs. This is qualitative, third-party-summarized data, not the structured OHLCV/technical-indicator/EDGAR feed used on other decision days — e.g. NVDA's 'current' price was restated two different ways ($211.80 vs $207.57) within a single search result. No new BUY/SELL/SHORT/COVER was sized off this data as a result; the existing AAPL position (opened 2026-07-14) was evaluated only against its own already-approved stop ($308) / take-profit ($338) plan, which doesn't require fresh numeric precision to check — see data/decisions.json for the full reasoning.

webSearchFindings.AAPL: Record high ~$321.29 on 2026-07-13, +16% since 2026-06-25. Catalysts: Broadcom chip supply deal extension through 2031 (announced 2026-07-06), on-device AI progress, CEO transition from Tim Cook to John Ternus, Citi price target raised to $365. Valuation ~34x forward earnings vs 10-yr avg 23x. Analyst consensus Moderate Buy, avg target $327.20. Earnings due 2026-07-30. Sources: tipranks.com, gurufocus.com, fxleaders.com, stockinvest.us (via WebSearch).
webSearchFindings.MSFT: Quoted ~$384.93 (2026-07-14 close), day range $377.35-$387.99. FQ2 2026: $81.3B revenue (+17% YoY), EPS $4.14, beat expectations. Catalysts: Copilot/365 AI rollout, Azure AI growth, Activision Blizzard gaming revenue. Source: WebSearch aggregated finance pages.

AAPL.status: Neither stop nor target hit per web-search price context (~$314-321 range referenced across sources) — held unchanged, no new decision needed.

decisionProcess: Applied the system prompt v2's 7-role deliberation (Technical Analyst -> Fundamentals Analyst -> News/Sentiment Analyst -> Bull Researcher -> Bear Researcher -> Trader synthesis -> Risk Manager sign-off) using the qualitative web-search findings above in place of the unreachable structured pipeline. The existing AAPL position remains within its original, already-risk-managed stop/target band and needed no action. AAPL, NVDA, JPM, and AVGO all show real bullish catalysts as fresh-entry candidates, but each had already made its move (record high, post-earnings pop, or deal-news pop) rather than offering a fresh, well-timed entry; AMZN and META carry a real fundamental headwind (heavy guided AI capex). No candidate combined a sound entry with data precise enough to compute a defensible Entry Price / Stop Loss / Take Profit / Risk-Reward Ratio, so the Risk Manager step vetoed every new-trade candidate.

entries._dataAsOf: 2026-07-14 (web search fallback — structured pipeline unreachable this session, see dataSourceNote)
